description: Selective High School Test - English Vocabulary word list

words.meaning: Departing from an accepted standard; abnormal or deviant.
words.example: The scientist noted the aberrant behaviour of the cells under the microscope.

words.meaning: To regard with disgust and hatred.
words.example: She abhors dishonesty and always insists on the truth.

words.meaning: Open to more than one interpretation; not clear or decided.
words.example: The instructions were so ambiguous that nobody knew what to do.

words.meaning: To make something bad or unsatisfactory better.
words.example: The new medicine helped ameliorate the patient's symptoms.

words.meaning: A thing belonging to a period other than that in which it exists.
words.example: A knight carrying a mobile phone would be a strange anachronism.

words.meaning: Involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring.
words.example: Climbing to the summit was an arduous journey that took three days.

words.meaning: Having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently.
words.example: The articulate student explained her project clearly to the judges.

words.meaning: Having the ability to accurately assess situations; shrewd.
words.example: The astute detective noticed the clue that everyone else had missed.

words.meaning: Showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks.
words.example: The audacious plan involved sneaking past the guards in broad daylight.

words.meaning: Well-meaning and kindly; generous.
words.example: The benevolent donor gave millions of dollars to the children's hospital.

words.meaning: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds.
words.example: The construction site produced a cacophony of drilling and hammering.

words.meaning: Truthful and straightforward; frank and honest.
words.example: She gave a candid assessment of the team's weaknesses.

words.meaning: Kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit.
words.example: The spies held a clandestine meeting in the basement of the old library.

words.meaning: Clear, logical, and convincing.
words.example: The lawyer made a cogent argument that persuaded the jury.

words.meaning: Having a superior attitude towards others; patronising.
words.example: His condescending tone made the students feel foolish for asking questions.

words.meaning: Having a meaning that is mysterious or obscure.
words.example: She left a cryptic note that none of us could understand.

words.meaning: Showing fearlessness and determination.
words.example: The dauntless firefighter rushed into the burning building without hesitation.

words.meaning: To make someone very weak and infirm.
words.example: The illness debilitated him so much that he could barely get out of bed.

words.meaning: Bleak and empty of people; feeling or showing misery.
words.example: After the floods, the town looked utterly desolate and abandoned.

words.meaning: Having or showing care and conscientiousness in work or duties.
words.example: The diligent student revised every night for three weeks before the exam.

words.meaning: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing.
words.example: Her eloquent speech moved the entire audience to tears.

words.meaning: Difficult to interpret or understand; mysterious.
words.example: The Mona Lisa's enigmatic smile has puzzled art lovers for centuries.

words.meaning: Lasting for a very short time.
words.example: The beauty of cherry blossoms is ephemeral, lasting only a week or two.

words.meaning: Open to more than one interpretation; deliberately ambiguous.
words.example: The politician gave an equivocal answer that satisfied nobody.

words.meaning: To make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse.
words.example: Scratching a mosquito bite will only exacerbate the irritation.

words.meaning: Having or displaying a passionate intensity.
words.example: She was a fervent supporter of environmental conservation.

words.meaning: Tending to attract attention because of exuberance and confidence.
words.example: The flamboyant performer wore a bright purple cape on stage.

words.meaning: Fond of company; sociable.
words.example: The gregarious child made friends with everyone at the new school.

words.meaning: Behaving in a way that contradicts one's stated beliefs.
words.example: It was hypocritical of him to preach honesty while hiding the truth.

words.meaning: Perfectly clean, neat, or tidy; free from mistakes.
words.example: The surgeon's technique was immaculate, leaving almost no scar.

words.meaning: In accordance with the highest standards; faultless.
words.example: Her impeccable manners impressed everyone at the formal dinner.

words.meaning: Not in harmony or keeping with the surroundings or context.
words.example: The modern skyscraper looked incongruous among the historic buildings.

words.meaning: Feeling or showing anger or annoyance at what is perceived as unfair treatment.
words.example: She was indignant when she was blamed for something she did not do.

words.meaning: Certain to happen; unavoidable.
words.example: With dark clouds gathering, rain seemed inevitable.

words.meaning: Showing great attention to detail or being very careful and precise.
words.example: The watchmaker was meticulous, fitting each tiny gear with steady hands.

words.meaning: Characterised by a vulgar or pretentious display; designed to impress.
words.example: The ostentatious mansion had gold taps in every bathroom.

words.meaning: Having a ready insight into things; shrewd.
words.example: The perspicacious investor sold her shares just before the market crashed.

words.meaning: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically rather than theoretically.
words.example: Instead of worrying, he took a pragmatic approach and solved the problem.

words.meaning: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions.
words.example: Despite many setbacks, she remained resilient and kept moving forward.

words.meaning: Holding firmly to something; very determined; not giving up.
words.example: The tenacious athlete trained every day despite the pain.
